ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: Yosef lale otii tinini di Yaa anu. Kaf…î…î atami…õ al…õ ookpee nw…î sinuns…î di batii anu. Nioso aabla disibu al…õ, oodi…õ nw…î bu…õ…õ.
translated: Yosef went to the farm with his mother. When they arrived, he saw a snake lying on the ground. As he tried to pick it up, it bit him.

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Di obe w…î di awosobi nwu nkpo …ît…õs…õbee lawo nii, kofe saate nwu nkpo lakpee bakp…õmblat…õ n…î…î al…õ baky…õ kofe nwu banyunt…õ nf≈© baalaaf≈© kalek…î n…î…î bawako nw…î.
translated: When a person dies in the month of Awosobi, their family must wait until the next month before they can perform the funeral rites, because it is forbidden to hold funerals during that month.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Diekye Yaa makanko ye f…õ mmle okle bi…õkanko ni batii. L…õklapoosaa le bi…õy…î bi…õklapoosa bi…õta ni bawo lee, nninwu ke di Yaa may…î anklapoosa anta ye n…õ.
translated: Yaa‚Äôs mother went to the farm and left her alone at home. A pot of soup was cooking on the fire, and Yaa‚Äôs mother told her not to touch the soup.

ref: But Jesus said, ‚ÄúLet the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.‚Äù
text: Ninf…õ di Yesu lat…îk…î basaateketet…õ nwu nkpo al…õ, ‚ÄúBita babis…î bawa …îky…õ nii, bitant≈©i ma osuku. Diekye batii f…õ babis…î ba mmle okle kale ninle Yaa s…õka kalek…î nwu nkpo.‚Äù
translated: Jesus told this parable to them: ‚ÄúA farmer went out to sow his seed. As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path and the birds came and ate it up.‚Äù

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu latofo disibu l…õma nwu, ni aat…îk…î ma al…õ, ‚ÄúS…õka kalek…î lele ni…õs…õ bawo di akudi akudi b…õ…õtaka bay…õ baawo suoto yi, manyila. Nkpo okle kaf…î…î se okpoo ee leyo lewo kam…õ batii l…õs…õ bawo akudi akudi b…î…îkp…õko bawo yi, mambi…õ.
translated: Jesus told this parable, saying, ‚ÄúA certain man planted a vineyard, leased it to tenants, and went away for a long time. When the time came to harvest the fruit, he sent a servant to the tenants to receive some of the fruit, but they beat him and sent him away empty-handed.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Nwaako kat…îk…î ye ni kaf…î…î, Ntu Kpeesat…õ Yohane l…õtaka katoo alenke otii lele nintoyisi…õ kayi ka mmle …õ…õbafe. Kaf…î…î otii w…î ninle ni …îy…õnt…õle di Yaa s…õka kalek…î nwu kam…õ yi, eekple …õ…õtaka katoo alenke Yohane nwu nkpo.
translated: When the house was completed, Ntu Kpeesat…õ Yohane invited everyone to come and celebrate the new home. Everyone came and saw that Yaa had built a beautiful house, and they all praised Yohane for the fine house he had built.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, ‚ÄúTalitha, koum!‚Äù which means, ‚ÄúLittle girl, get up!‚Äù The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Nfanwu …îsankobi nwu lataka aaky…õ. Lefosi al…õ…õ any…î obi aale. Diat…õ diabla batii nwu …îkp…õ osie kanya.
translated: The hunter‚Äôs son died a long time ago. Lefosi has married someone else. Diat…õ‚Äôs child has gone to the farm.

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Nfanwu, Yesu lakyakako ma di osuku. Ni aat…îk…î ma al…õ, ‚ÄúAtoko …îky…õ ole disi…õko ye.‚Äù Ninf…õ baakpukuti baat…õ…õt…õ nw…î, baamuf≈© nw…î nkpaa, baasumu nw…î n…õ.
translated: Jesus was born in Bethlehem. When they arrived, they said, "There is no room here." So they went and looked for a place, found a cave, and entered it.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, ‚ÄúNothing like this has ever happened in Israel!‚Äù
text: Ni di Yesu latososa ninwuna kpile nwu nkpo aadiki di omumu nwu kam…õ n…õ. Nfanwu ni aakyako …ît…î…îkakat…õ n…õ. L…õsaa le mmle labla batii ba bamuu niayila ni ninfa yaaa, ni baabu…õ al…õ, ‚ÄúBuunya nle mmle okle di Israel kas…î suoto bunya!‚Äù
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd around him, he felt deep compassion for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. Then he said to his disciples, ‚ÄúThe harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field!‚Äù

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Nse beetikanko ye di okpoo onwii kam…õ yi, bietoso bisifi …îbamba kam…õ. Nwaako k…ît…îk…î ye nl…õ bilabaloo s…õkp…õ lee …îbla di Israel sekpoo simuu kam…õ fi…õ di ami, Otii Obi nwu, makple anwa.
translated: The elders say that death is not something to be feared, but rather something to be accepted. No one knows the day or hour when the Lord will call them, just as the people of Israel did not know when the Lord would take them, says Otii Obi, my grandfather.
